content: The correspondence of Yajnavalkya's words with the text of Manu is very close, suggesting that Yajnavalkya had the Manusmriti before him and attempted to abridge Manu's expressions [1]. This indicates that both texts share similar content and ideas, with Yajnavalkya's work possibly building upon or responding to Manu's.
